title: Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur
tagline: Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur, is a Siva temple in Tiruvekambathur in Ramnad District in Tamil Nadu (India).
overview.description: Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur, is a Siva temple in Tiruvekambathur in Ramnad District in Tamil Nadu (India). It is also known as Tiruvekambattu and Tiruvekambam.

topPlaces.name: Kavadukudi
topPlaces.description: Kavadukudi is a village in the Avadaiyarkoil revenue block of Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Erwadi fire incident
topPlaces.description: The Erwadi fire incident was an incident that occurred on 6 August 2001, when 28 inmates of a faith-based mental asylum died in the fire. All these inmates were bound by chains at the Moideen Badusha Mental Home in Erwadi village in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Kalkulam taluk
topPlaces.description: Kallkkulam taluk is a taluk of Kanyakumari district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Kallkkulam.Up to 1957, Kallkkulam Taluk formed part of the Travancore Kingdom and subsequently the Travancore-Cochin State.

topPlaces.name: Odaikkarai
topPlaces.description: Odaikkarai is a village in Tiruvadanai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located near the southern tip of the Indian subcontinent, about 43 kilometres north of the district seat Ramanathapuram, and 15 kilometres west of the taluk seat Tiruvadanai.

items.note: Kavadukudi is a village in the Avadaiyarkoil revenue block of Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

The Erwadi fire incident was an incident that occurred on 6 August 2001, when 28 inmates of a faith-based mental asylum died in the fire. All these inmates were bound by chains at the Moideen Badusha Mental Home in Erwadi village in Tamil Nadu, India.

Kallkkulam taluk is a taluk of Kanyakumari district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Kallkkulam.Up to 1957, Kallkkulam Taluk formed part of the Travancore Kingdom and subsequently the Travancore-Cochin State.

Odaikkarai is a village in Tiruvadanai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located near the southern tip of the Indian subcontinent, about 43 kilometres north of the district seat Ramanathapuram, and 15 kilometres west of the taluk seat Tiruvadanai.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (76 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (73 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur?
faq.a: Stays in Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ekambaranathar Temple, Tiruvekambathur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kavadukudi, Erwadi fire incident, Kalkulam taluk, Odaikkarai.
